channel.title: Art on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Art on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Industry Changes
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about industry changes because there have been a lot of stuff going on: a lot of layoffs recently, a lot of concern about AI taking jobs.

People are freaking out and let me just make this clear at the beginning, so no one takes this all out of context later, even though I know some people are. It is bad to lose your job. I’ve lost my job, uh, in the past. It’s bad when you lose your job. That is not what I’m talking about here. I’m talking about news and YouTube, pundits and, you know, industry folks saying the industry is collapsing, it’s collapsing, it’s, horrible.

item.title: Challenges Facing Game Artists
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to finish my series on challenges that game developers face by talking about the challenges that artists face, and I almost didn’t do this one because I know

this is going to be hard to believe, but me, a colorblind programmer, by training, have never done art in a computer game. I tried. I tried to put a mouse cursor into Rags to Riches, but it was caught and people pointed out this is a horrible mouse cursor. I thought it was pretty cool, and it was removed.
